Undid revision 1010158400 by [[Special:Contributions/Giraffedata|Giraffedata]] ([[User talk:Giraffedata|talk]]) "comprise" is the broader term (per Webster - comprise: "to be made up of; COMPOSE, CONSTITUTE;" compose: "to form by putting together; FASHION; to form the substance of; CONSTITUTE); denotatively, "compose" has a more intentional element while "comprise: is more [[ergative]]; practically speaking, however, I doubt most people can construe the difference

Let's be more cautious... A google search shows that the ""smallest proteins" have less than 20 aminoacids. And Amyloid-beta, a polypeptide of 36–43 amino acids, is considered to be a peptide.....

A protein contains at least one long polypeptide. Short polypeptides, containing less than about 20-30 residues, are not considered to be proteins and are commonly called [[peptide]]s, or sometimes oligopeptides.
[[peptide|Short polypeptides]], containing less than about 50 aminoacid residues, are typically not regarded as proteins. (See [[Peptide]]).
